text: Git branching is an essential part of version control that helps teams work on projects without interfering with each other’s code. When developers need to add a new feature or fix a bug, they create a feature branch from the main code. This lets them experiment safely. Once the work is complete, they merge the changes back into the main branch. In many workflows, this is done through a pull request. A pull request allows team members to review the code, discuss improvements, and ensure quality before the merge happens. This structured version control workflow keeps projects organized and reduces the risk of conflicts.
